hypothesis: Correcting aromatic valency handling and training 3D molecule generative models on a Kekulized, chemically accurate GEOM-Drugs dataset will reduce chemically invalid outputs and produce generated structures with significantly smaller bond/angle/torsion deviations (Δr, Δθ, Δφ) and lower relaxation energies (ΔE_relax) under GFN2-xTB optimization than models trained on the original dataset, and GFN2-xTB-based evaluation will report substantially lower and more reliable relaxation energies than MMFF94, demonstrating its superior fidelity for benchmarking geometric accuracy.
